lecture_topics: Ashby plots: strength vs. service temperature
Fracture energy in building codes (Northridge, Liberty ships)
Honda Civic corrosion failure and reputation recovery
Externalities of safety regulation (fire watch, copper pipe crimp fittings)
Massachusetts police/fire details and living wage
Beverage container materials competition (steel, aluminum, plastic, glass)
Pineapple juice composite can; calculus of variations
Plastic gas tanks (terne-coated steel → 7-layer composites; EPA permeability)
Beer purists and glass; co-located glass plants and breweries
Water and gas pipe history (clay, wood, cast iron, plastic, stainless)
Corrugated stainless steel tubing and lightning-strike fires
flags_for_editorial_register: p4/p5: Tom dates Griffith's equation to 1925 in p4 and '1945' in p5; the canonical date is 1920/1921 (Phil. Trans. Roy. Soc., 'The phenomena of rupture and flow in solids'). Both dates preserved per convention §16; editor may wish to footnote in layer 3.
p27: 'the Steve— Japan' — verbal stumble; unclear whether Tom was about to say a name (Steve Jobs?) or just stumbled. Preserved as written.
p36: 'twenty-five years ago I had a student working at Ford' — places Tom's Ford-paint-line consulting circa 1992 if lecture is 2017. Verifiable against Tom's consulting record.
p54: Tom names co-instructor as 'Brian, or Simone, Dr. Belmar' — verify which colleague (likely a Dr. Belmar; first name uncertain from audio).
